NOVEMBER 28, 2025\|4 MINUTE READ

# The Best Artificial Intelligence Conferences & Events of 2026

By [Samantha Dunn](https://www.splunk.com/en_us/blog/author/sdunn.html)

- [![Share on X](https://www.splunk.com/icons/x-social.svg)](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Found%20this%20useful%20link%20for%20you.%20%23splunk&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.splunk.com%2Fen_us%2Fblog%2Flearn%2Fai-artificial-intelligence-conferences-events.html "Share on X")
- [![Share on Facebook](https://www.splunk.com/icons/facebook-social.svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.splunk.com%2Fen_us%2Fblog%2Flearn%2Fai-artificial-intelligence-conferences-events.html&title=The%20Best%20Artificial%20Intelligence%20Conferences%20%26%20Events%20of%202026 "Share on Facebook")
- [![Share on LinkedIn](https://www.splunk.com/icons/linkedin-social.svg)](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.splunk.com%2Fen_us%2Fblog%2Flearn%2Fai-artificial-intelligence-conferences-events.html&title=The%20Best%20Artificial%20Intelligence%20Conferences%20%26%20Events%20of%202026&summary=Found%20this%20useful%20link%20for%20you. "Share on LinkedIn")

# The Best Artificial Intelligence Conferences &\#x26; Events of 2026

Artificial intelligence is continuing to shape the future of industries, from healthcare and finance to manufacturing and education. In 2026, experts, innovators, and thought leaders will gather at a host of conferences and events across the globe to share the latest trends, breakthroughs, and best practices in the field.

Whether you are looking to connect with AI professionals at an intimate regional meetup or attend a large-scale global summit, we have curated a comprehensive guide to the most anticipated AI conferences and events of 2026. These gatherings provide unparalleled opportunities to deepen your knowledge, network with key players, and explore emerging technologies.

If you have an AI conference or event that you’d like to see included in our list, please reach out via email to [sadunn@cisco.com](mailto:sadunn@cisco.com "sadunn@cisco.com") with details, including the conference name, dates, location, and website.

Without further ado, here is your ultimate guide to the best AI conferences and events of 2026!

## AI conferences and events

### [AAAI 2026 (Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence)](https://aaai.org/conference/aaai/aaai-26/ "AAAI 2026 (Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence)")

**Dates:** January 20-27, 2026

**Location:** Singapore

**Cost:** Check the website

AAAI 2026 brings together the global AI research community to discuss advances in artificial intelligence theory and practice. Experience a diverse program featuring technical papers, invited talks, workshops, and competitions. Network with leading academics and industry professionals while exploring the latest trends in AI.

### [Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/data-analytics-us "Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026")

**Dates:** March 9-11, 2026

**Location:** Orlando, Florida

**Cost:** Starting at $4,475

The Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026 is a premier event for AI leaders and data professionals. Explore how AI, data, and analytics are converging to transform business strategies and drive innovation. Attend sessions on AI governance, generative AI, and data fabric, and gain actionable insights from Gartner analysts and industry experts.

### [NVIDIA GTC AI Conference](https://www.nvidia.com/gtc/ "NVIDIA GTC AI Conference")

**Dates:** March 16-19, 2026

**Location:** San Jose, California

**Cost:** Starting at $2,172

NVIDIA GTC 2026 is a premier global AI conference bringing together developers, researchers, business leaders, and innovators. Experience keynote addresses from NVIDIA’s leadership, technical sessions on cutting-edge AI, deep learning, and accelerated computing, as well as hands-on training and workshops. Whether you’re interested in breakthroughs in generative AI, robotics, or edge computing, GTC offers unparalleled opportunities to network and learn from industry trailblazers.

### [HumanX](https://www.humanx.co/ "HumanX")

**Dates:** April 6-9, 2026

**Location:** San Francisco, California

**Cost:** Starting at $2,150

HumanX 2026 brings together global leaders, innovators, and practitioners to explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and human potential. Dive into keynote sessions, interactive workshops, and networking opportunities focused on AI for good, human-centered design, and the ethical advancement of technology.

### [SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit 2026](https://www.sans.org/cyber-security-training-events/ai-summit-2026 "SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit 2026")

**Dates:** April 20-27, 2026

**Location:** Arlington, Virginia + Virtual

**Cost:** In-Person $525 (virtual is free)

For cybersecurity professionals eager to leverage artificial intelligence (AI) in their work, the SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit 2026 is a must-attend event. With hands-on workshops and live demos, this summit offers practical, real-world skills to integrate AI and machine learning (ML) into your cybersecurity tasks for greater efficiency and effectiveness.

From applying AI in incident response and cyber defense to defending and attacking AI systems, you will gain actionable strategies you can apply immediately. Attend in person in Arlington, or virtually, for an opportunity to learn from experts, network with peers, and take your cybersecurity practices to the next level.

### [AI Con USA](https://aiconusa.techwell.com/ "AI Con USA")

**Dates:** June 7-12, 2026

**Location:** Seattle, Washington

**Cost:** Starting at $1,795

Attend AI Con USA in Seattle to explore the latest in AI and machine learning. With keynotes, sessions on topics like generative AI and MLOps, and hands-on training, this event is perfect for professionals eager to stay ahead. Network with experts, discover innovative solutions at the Expo, and dive into the future of AI across industries like healthcare, finance, and more. Don’t miss the opportunity to advance your knowledge and career.

### [Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026](https://www.databricks.com/dataaisummit "Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026")

**Dates:** June 15-18, 2026

**Location:** San Francisco, California

**Cost:** TBD

The Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026 is a must-attend event for data professionals. Over four days, explore 700+ sessions, keynotes, and workshops on topics like data engineering, machine learning, Delta Lake, and Apache Spark™. Join 20,000+ peers to discover the latest AI technologies, gain hands-on training, and network with experts. Take your skills to the next level with certifications and connect with the global data and AI community.

### [The AI Summit London](https://london.theaisummit.com/ "The AI Summit London")

**Dates:** June 10-11, 2026

**Location:** London, England

**Cost:** Starting at £2,399

The AI Summit London 2026, taking place June 10-11 at Tobacco Dock, is the flagship event of London Tech Week. With 300+ speakers and 100+ tech companies, explore AI’s real-world impact across industries. Network with 4,500+ attendees, discover cutting-edge solutions at the Expo, and attend workshops and masterclasses. Join us to accelerate AI-driven growth and connect with industry leaders shaping the future of AI.

### [SuperAI](https://www.superai.com/ "SuperAI")

**Dates:** June 10-11, 2026

**Location:** Marina Bay Sands, Singapore

**Cost:** Starting at $399 (up to $2,999)

Join 10,000+ attendees at the SuperAI Conference 2025 in Singapore this June. With 150+ speakers, 1,500+ companies, and top AI partners like Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI, it’s the perfect place for networking and discovering the latest in AI innovation. Don’t miss your chance to connect with industry leaders and shape the future of AI — register now with the Early Bird discount.

### [AI World Congress 2026](https://aiconference.london/ "AI World Congress 2026")

**Dates:** June 23-24, 2026

**Location:** London, United Kingdom

**Cost:** Starting at £495 for early bird pricing

The AI World Congress 2026 stands as Europe's premier gathering for senior level decision makers artificial intelligence and robotics innovation. This flagship event brings together global leaders, researchers, and enterprises exploring the convergence of AI and robotics technologies transforming industries worldwide. Major AI brands will be represented, with speakers from Anthropic, Microsoft, IBM, and more.

### [Ai4](https://ai4.io/vegas/ "Ai4")

**Dates:** August 4-6, 2026

**Location:** Las Vegas, Nevada

**Cost:** Starting at $1,695

Ai4 2025, North America’s largest AI event, brings together 12,000+ attendees from August 4-6 at The Venetian, Las Vegas. With 1,000+ speakers and 400+ exhibitors, explore the latest in AI innovation, including generative AI and AI agents. Network with leaders across industries and accelerate your AI initiatives at this must-attend event.

### [The AI Conference](https://aiconference.com/ "The AI Conference")

**Dates:** September 30 – October 1, 2026

**Location:** San Francisco, California

**Cost:** TBD

Attend the two day in-person AI Conference from September 30th to October 1st in San Francisco. This premier event explores key AI topics like AGI, generative AI, ethics, and startups. Engage with top AI experts, discover cutting-edge research, and network with pioneers shaping the future of AI. Don’t miss this opportunity to stay ahead in the evolving AI landscape.

### [AI Expo Europe 2026](https://www.ai-expo.net/europe/ "AI Expo Europe 2026")

**Dates:** October 20-21, 2026

**Location:** Amsterdam, Netherlands

**Cost:** TBD

AI Expo Europe 2026 connects technology enthusiasts, business leaders, and developers at one of the continent’s largest AI events. Explore the latest solutions in machine learning, NLP, and AI-driven automation through talks, demos, and networking sessions. Discover how organizations are deploying AI to solve real-world challenges across industries.

## Wrapping up

Whether you choose to attend an in-person or virtual AI event in 2026, there are many options to choose from to learn the latest best practices and frameworks.

If you know of another great AI or machine learning conference we should add to the list, don’t hesitate to send a suggestion to the author of this post at [sadunn@cisco.com](mailto:sadunn@cisco.com "sadunn@cisco.com") with details including the conference URL, conference name, dates of the event, and location details.

See an error or have a suggestion? Please let us know by emailing

[splunkblogs@cisco.com](mailto:splunkblogs@cisco.com "splunkblogs@cisco.com").

_This posting does not necessarily represent Splunk's position, strategies or opinion._

## In this article

- [🔷 MCP-Specific Events](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#mcp-specific-events)
- [🤖 Pure AI & General AI Conferences](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#pure-ai-general-ai-conferences)
- [🏢 Big Tech Developer Conferences](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#big-tech-developer-conferences)
- [🔧 Developer & Platform Ecosystem Conferences](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#developer-platform-ecosystem-conferences)
- [🏛️ Enterprise AI Strategy](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#enterprise-ai-strategy)
- [🛡️ Security & Governance Conferences](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#security-governance-conferences)
- [💻 Conclusion: Don’t Forget About Local Meetups and Hackathons](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/ai-conferences-list/#conclusion-dont-forget-about-local-meetups-and-hackathons)

AI moves at a whiplash-inducing speed. Nearly every week there is some game-changing new development. To stay on top of things, AI enthusiasts and engineers remain consistently immersed in product announcements, while also tinkering with new AI advancements. However, it’s also important to get out of day-to-day news cycles and experimentation; being around other AI experts can offer a more elevated perspective and actionable advice.

That’s exactly what AI conferences offer. To top it off, you also gain community amongst like-minded individuals. These are the MCP and AI conferences worth checking out in 2026, many of which also offer live streaming options.

## 🔷 MCP-Specific Events

MCP has become the standard way for connecting AI to external tools and data. Here are the top events within the MCP community.

- **[MCPconference London](https://www.mcp-conference.com/) \+ [ContainerDays London](https://www.containerdays.io/containerdays-london-2026/)** _February 11–12, 2026 \| London_ _\|_ The first stop in the MCPconference global series, co-located with ContainerDays London. MCPconference also has other stops throughout the year (including an NYC event on March 11, along with Paris, Berlin, Hamburg, San Jose, Singapore.) This event brings together the people building with MCP, with talks ranging from agent frameworks and tooling to enterprise integrations and production infrastructure.

- **[Agentics Day: MCP + Agents](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/kubecon-cloudnativecon-europe/co-located-events/agentics-day-mcp-agents/)**(CNCF / Linux Foundation) _March 23, 2026 \| Amsterdam, Netherlands_ _\|_ A half-day event co-located with KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe. This event is focused on real-world MCP implementations, security, and enterprise integration. Speakers include MCP core maintainers and MCP experts from companies like Anthropic, Block, and more.

- **[MCP Dev Summit NYC](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/mcp-dev-summit-north-america/)** _April 2–3, 2026 \| New York City_ _\|_ This is The Linux Foundation’s flagship MCP event for 2026. With nearly 50 speakers from companies like Google, Goose, and Anthropic, attendees can expect actionable advice on production implementations, security, and enterprise integration. This is highest-profile dedicated MCP gathering in North America.

> Shameless plug alert: I will be speaking at MCP Dev Summit NYC. My talk is “How to Scale MCP Without Skipping Compliance.”

![mcp dev summit speaker promotion](https://mcpmanager.ai/wp-content/uploads/Untitled-design-13.png)

- **[MCP Dev Summit Europe](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/mcp-dev-summit-europe/)**(Linux Foundation) _Sept 17-19, 2026 \| Amsterdam, Netherlands \|_ After Anthropic donated MCP to The Linux Foundation, they confirmed a slate of 2026 programming around MCP. This is the the Europe counterpart to the NYC event.

- **[MCP Connect Day](https://luma.com/alpic)** _Ongoing, various dates_ and locations _\|_ This is a recurring, community-organized series organized by [Alpic](https://alpic.ai/). These events are lower-key but consistently active; they are a good pulse check on grassroots MCP adoption and innovation. In Q1 of 2026, they hosted events in Paris and NYC. Check out [Alpic’s Twitter](https://x.com/alpic_ai) or LinkedIn for updates or [follow them on Luma](https://luma.com/alpic) (where they post events).

## 🤖 Pure AI & General AI Conferences

These flagship AI events are for a high-level and broad audience. They also have a major industry impact.

- **[Cisco AI Summit](https://www.ciscoaisummit.com/)** _January 2026 \| Virtual / On-Demand l_ High-profile keynotes from Jensen Huang, Sam Altman, Marc Andreessen, Fei-Fei Li, and more. Available on-demand for those who missed it.

![Video Preview](https://privacy-proxy-server.usercentrics.eu/video/youtube/-1allyNRNG4-poster-image)

### We need your consent to load the YouTube Video service!

We use a third party service to embed video content that may collect data about your activity. Please review the details and accept the service to watch this video.

More InformationAccept

powered by [Usercentrics Consent Management Platform](https://usercentrics.com/consent-management-platform-powered-by-usercentrics/)

- [Developer Week](https://www.developerweek.com/) _February 18-20, 2026 \| San Jose, CA_ _l_ The world’s largest AI engineering conference and expo. They also have a popular Hackaton where thousands of dollars of prizes are given out. In addition, they have speakers from leading engineering companies like Oracle, Google, Amazon, and more.

- **[Optimized AI Conference (OAI)](https://www.oaiconference.com/)** _March 30–31, 2026 \| Atlanta, GA_ _l_ A practitioner-first, hands-on event for engineers and data scientists building and deploying AI in production. Covers agentic systems, LLM evals, observability, and multi-agent architectures. Affordable ($250 tickets) with speakers from Google, NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Netflix.

- **[HumanX](https://humanx.co/)** _April 6–9, 2026 \| San Francisco, CA \|_Sometimes called the “Davos of AI,” this conference focuses on high-level topics like AI governance, strategy, safety, and enterprise transformation. 6,500+ CEOs, policymakers, investors, and enterprise leaders attend. There are five different tracks for attendees: Builders (product, engineering), Command Desk (corporate strategy, legal & compliance), Control Room (finance, HR), Customer Engine (marketing, sales) and Ecosystem (VC and startups).
  - HumanX also holds the [event in Europe](https://www.humanx.co/europe) in Amsterdam, Netherlands on September 24-26.

- **[Ai4](https://ai4.io/)** _August 4–6, 2026 \| Las Vegas, NV \|_ The self-proclaimed “largest AI conference in America”, AI4 boasts 12,000 attendees, 1,000 speakers, and 400 exhibitors. Attendees and speakers are primarily Fortune 500 IT and business decision-makers across financial services, healthcare, cybersecurity, manufacturing, and more.

- **[The AI Conference](https://aiconference.com/)** _September 29–October 1, 2026 \| San Francisco, CA \|_ A vendor-neutral, deeply technical conference covering AGI, LLMs, agents, infrastructure, and applied A, this conference offers five tracks: AI Frontiers, AI Developers, AI Strategy, Applied AI, and AI Infrastructure. Roughly 5,500+ builders, researchers, and leaders attend each year.

- **[World Summit AI](https://worldsummit.ai/)** _October 7–8, 2026 \| Amsterdam, Netherlands_ _\|_ The 10th anniversary edition of one of the oldest and most globally recognized AI summits. Anchors “World AI Week” (Oct 5–9), a broader ecosystem of 100+ satellite events across Amsterdam. There are other World Summits in other continents throughout the year, as well.

## 🏢 Big Tech Developer Conferences

These vendor-hosted developer events have become de facto AI conferences. These events are helpful for tracking where the major platforms are taking their AI and MCP tooling.

- **[NVIDIA GTC](https://www.nvidia.com/gtc/)** _March 16–19, 2026 \| San Jose, CA_ _\|_ Often called the “Woodstock of AI,” this premier global conference is for developers, researchers, and enterprise IT leaders. Talks center on AI infrastructure, physical AI, agentic systems, and inference. Jensen Huang’s keynote alone sets industry direction for the year. 25,000+ in-person attendees, 300,000+ virtual.

- **[Google Cloud Next](http://googlecloudevents.com/next-vegas)** _April 22–24, 2026 \| Las Vegas, NV_ _\|_ Google’s flagship cloud and AI event, attendees can expect major Gemini, Vertex AI, and agent-related announcements. Heavily attended by enterprise IT and cloud architects, this event is also live streamed.

- **[Atlassian Team ’26](https://events.atlassian.com/team)** _May 5–7, 2026 \| Anaheim, CA_ _\|_ Atlassian is explicitly marketing this as “the global conference for AI-forward teams.” Rovo AI will be front and center, along with other [Atlassian’s MCP server](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/atlassian-mcp-server-installation-best-practices/) and other AI features that Atlassian will roll out. Team is a highly relevant event for teams tracking AI-powered productivity and collaboration tooling.

[![atlassian team](https://mcpmanager.ai/wp-content/uploads/atlassian-team-2048x814.png)](https://events.atlassian.com/team)

- [Microsoft Build](https://build.microsoft.com/) _TBD date & location \|_ Because Microsoft’s annual developer conference has moved out of Seattle, no new dates or location have been announced. However, this conference has historically occurred in May. Expect deep coverage of Copilot, Azure AI, and MCP-related tooling.

- **[Google I/O](https://io.google/2026)** _May 19–20, 2026 \| Mountain View, CA \|_ Google’s primary developer conference, sessions focus on Gemini, Android, AI in Chrome and Workspace, and agentic developer tools. Free to watch online; limited in-person attendance by invite or lottery.

- **[Salesforce Dreamforce](https://salesforce.com/dreamforce)** _September 15–17, 2026 \| San Francisco, CA \|_ Salesforce’s massive annual conference draws 40,000+ in-person attendees. In 2026, Agentforce will dominate the agenda, which is Salesforce’s push into AI agents is directly adjacent to the broader MCP ecosystem conversation.

- **[AWS re:Invent](https://reinvent.awsevents.com/)** _Late November / Early December, 2026 \| Las Vegas, NV \|_ This is one of the largest developer conferences in the world at ~60,000 attendees. AWS has been actively building out MCP and agentic AI tooling through Amazon Bedrock and the Strands Agents SDK, making this a key event for tracking enterprise AI and MCP adoption heading into 2027.

## 🔧 Developer & Platform Ecosystem Conferences

These events center on the platforms and infrastructure where AI and MCP adoption is happening.

- **[KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/kubecon-cloudnativecon-europe)** _March 23–26, 2026 \| Amsterdam, Netherlands \|_ The cloud-native community’s flagship event. MCP in enterprise environments increasingly lives in Kubernetes and containerized infrastructure, making this directly relevant territory. The co-located Agentics Day (see MCP section above) also happens here.

- **[Databricks Data + AI Summit](https://dataaisummit.databricks.com/)** _June 15–18, 2026 \| San Francisco, CA \|_ One of the world’s largest data and AI gatherings with 70,000+ registrants and 700+ sessions. There’s a strong overlap with the enterprise data and AI engineering audience. Topics include data governance, AI pipelines, Unity Catalog, and open-source AI tooling.

- **[KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/kubecon-cloudnativecon-north-america)** _November 9–12, 2026 \| Salt Lake City, UT \|_ The North American edition of KubeCon, you can expect MCP-in-production discussions, security sessions, and cloud-native AI integration content. This event is particularly relevant for enterprise IT and platform engineering audiences.

## 🏛️ Enterprise AI Strategy

These vendor-neutral forums are for decision-makers, buyers, and leaders building and governing AI at scale.

- **[Agentic & Applied AI for the Enterprise](https://ssonetwork.com/events-agentic-applied-ai-enterprise)** _June 8-10 \| Atlanta, GA \|_ A practitioner-first event with four tracks: Enterprise Strategy & Metrics, Orchestration & Lifecycle Management, Governance & Data Foundations, and Human & AI Collaboration. This event focuses on the hard work of moving organizations from AI pilots to scalable, accountable systems.

- **[The AI Enterprise Conference](https://datasciconnect.com/)**(Data Science Connect) _September 2, 2026 \| New York City \|_ Positioned as the nation’s largest enterprise-focused data & AI event, this event targets Fortune 500 data and AI leaders with sessions on operationalizing AI, ROI, and governance. Sessions are practical, rather than theoretical.

- **[Reuters Events: Momentum AI Austin](https://events.reutersevents.com/momentum)** _September 24–25, 2026 \| Austin, TX \|_ A tightly curated, 500-person forum built exclusively for enterprise AI decision-makers. Deliberately high-signal, with roundtables and workshops designed for candid peer exchange. This isn’t a mass-attendance expo but rather a more intimate gathering

## 🛡️ Security & Governance Conferences

These conferences are focused on AI risk, compliance, and enterprise security.

- **[IANS Symposium: MCP Risks & Opportunities](https://www.iansresearch.com/what-we-do/events/all-events)** _February 19, 2026 \| Virtual \|_ Security-focused, for IANS clients and invited guests only — no vendor presence. The fact that this event existed at all is a strong signal that CISOs and security researchers are actively analyzing MCP-specific risk.

- **[RSA Conference](https://rsaconference.com/)** _March 23–26, 2026 \| San Francisco, CA \|_ The world’s largest cybersecurity conference, drawing nearly 44,000 attendees, 730+ speakers, and 650+ exhibitors. In 2026, agentic AI, identity security, and AI governance are dominating the agenda. Notably, the Innovation Sandbox this year features multiple finalists building specifically for AI agent security and governance.

- **[Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit](https://gartner.com/)** _June 2026 \| National Harbor, MD \|_ A strategy-first conference led by Gartner analysts covering AI-driven risk modeling, zero trust, identity-first security, and resilience metrics. Less about tools, more about board-level frameworks and governance roadmaps.

- **[Infosecurity Europe](https://infosecurityeurope.com/)** _June 2026 \| London \|_ One of the most respected cybersecurity gatherings in the EMEA region, celebrating its 30th anniversary. Combines a large exhibition floor with expert-led tracks on AI defense, cloud security, and identity management. The go-to event for European market reach in this space.

- **[Black Hat USA](https://blackhat.com/)** _August 2026 \| Las Vegas, NV_ _\|_ The technically rigorous counterpart to RSA includes deep research sessions, executive tracks, and a focus on emerging attack vectors.

## 💻 Conclusion: Don’t Forget About Local Meetups and Hackathons

If flying out to a big conferences feels overwhelming, look at places like Luma and Meetup.com to find AI community events in your area. There are also virtual options.

The grassroots tier of the AI and MCP ecosystem is often where builders experiment, prototype, and push the protocol forward. New events are being added regularly. Here are just a few notable MCP-related events I found while perusing Luma and Google:

- **[MCP AI Hackathon](https://luma.com/ll67dtzf) –** San Francisco _\|_ May 1, 2026 _\|_ A one-day hackathon focused on building with MCP.
- [**MCP Official Working Groups**](https://meet.modelcontextprotocol.io/) – Virtual _\|_ The official MCP spec has ongoing working groups that you can join to see where the protocol is headed.
- **[MCP Lisbon Meetup](https://lu.ma/7nuc7uau)** **–** A community event focused on enterprise-readiness, auth, and observability for MCP deployments.
- **[MCP and A2A Hackathon](http://lu.ma/vibecode) –** AWS Edition _\|_ May 2, 2026 _\|_ Hosted at the AWS Startup Loft SF.
- **[MCP x CPH](https://meetup.com/mcp-x-cph)** (Copenhagen) An ongoing meetup series for the Danish market with a business and technology focus.

And of course, you can always find webinars and educational videos online, including one of our most popular webinars about [MCP gateways](https://mcpmanager.ai/blog/mcp-gateway/).

# AI for Agents Workshop

[![Photo of the user Mark Hinkle](https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/member/b/a/c/c/thumb_320927820.jpeg?w=96)\\
\\

## Details   simpleHorizontalLine scribble

**Every AI agent is a delegation decision. This workshop teaches you how to make those decisions well.**

\[To purchase tickets, visit [https://2026.allthingsai.org/register](https://2026.allthingsai.org/register) workshop tickets also include access to the All Things AI conference on March 24th. _**Price increases after Tuesday, March 17 so sign-up early and save $100**_ \]

Many organizations have experimented with AI Agents. Far fewer have moved confidently into sustained, responsible execution.

As teams progress from early pilots to real-world deployment, leaders often encounter the same challenges: impressive prototypes that stall before production, uncertainty about where agents actually add value, late-breaking security and governance concerns, and misalignment between business and technical stakeholders about what “success” should look like. Too often, these issues surface only after cost, complexity, or risk have already compounded.

This workshop helps leaders make the shift from experimentation to execution—not by pushing agents everywhere, but by providing a clear blueprint for **when agents make sense, when they don’t, and how to make sound decisions when autonomy is on the table**.

\\*\\*\\*

\#### **The Outcome: Better Decisions, Earlier**

By the end of this session, participants will have more than an overview of AI Agents. They will leave with a **practical decision-making framework** they can apply immediately to real initiatives.

You’ll be able to:

- determine whether an agent is appropriate for a given problem—or whether a copilot or traditional automation is the better choice
- evaluate agent proposals with a clear understanding of autonomy, risk, and tradeoffs
- anticipate the organizational, architectural, and governance factors that separate successful deployments from stalled experiments
- identify common failure modes early—while they are still inexpensive to address

The result is clearer judgment about **what to delegate to agents, what to constrain, and what should remain human**.

\\*\\*\\*

\#### **What the Blueprint Covers**

In this intensive workshop, business and technical leaders will work through a practical blueprint for AI Agent success, grounded in real-world constraints and leadership realities.

Key topics include:

- understanding degrees of agency and how they change cost, risk, and accountability
- distinguishing agents from copilots and traditional automation—and why that distinction matters
- identifying use cases where agents consistently create value, and scenarios where they introduce unnecessary fragility
- recognizing common pitfalls that derail agent initiatives, from over-delegation to hidden operational complexity
- aligning organizational readiness, security, and governance with the level of autonomy being introduced

Participants will also receive a high-level technical orientation to the AI Agent ecosystem, including platforms, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and model selection tradeoffs. This includes a balanced discussion of the benefits and risks of using Small Language Models (SLMs) in agentic workflows, particularly when autonomy and tool access are involved.

\\*\\*\\*

\#### **What You’ll Leave With**

Participants will leave with:

- A clear framework for deciding **if and when** AI Agents should be used
- A practical blueprint outlining the conditions required for agent success—and the signals that indicate trouble
- A realistic understanding of the cost, complexity, and operational demands of agent-based systems
- Clear guidance for incorporating security, governance, and human oversight from the start
- A shared language that aligns business and technical leaders around the same decision criteria

\\*\\*\\*

\#### **Who This Is For**

This workshop is designed for business and technical leaders responsible for turning AI experimentation into operational reality, including:

- Leaders driving digital, AI, or transformation initiatives (e.g., Chief Digital Officers, Heads of AI, Transformation and Digital Strategy leaders)
- Technology and architecture leaders accountable for execution and scale (e.g., CTOs, CIOs, VPs of Engineering, Enterprise and Solution Architects)
- Product and innovation leaders shaping AI-enabled capabilities (e.g., CPOs, Heads of Product, Innovation and Emerging Technology leaders)
- Security, risk, and governance leaders involved in overseeing autonomous or AI-driven systems (e.g., CISOs, Responsible AI and Risk leaders)

This session is especially valuable for leaders who sit at the intersection of strategy and execution—those expected to decide **when AI Agents should be used, how much authority they should have, and how to govern them responsibly**.

\[To purchase tickets, visit [https://2026.allthingsai.org/register](https://2026.allthingsai.org/register) workshop tickets also include access to the All Things AI conference on March 24th. _**Price increases after Tuesday, March 17 so sign-up early and save $100**_ \]

### Speaker

![Speaker](https://secure-content.meetupstatic.com/images/classic-events/532347699/100x100.jpg)

#### Don Shin

Don Shin is Founder and CEO of CrossComm, an award-winning development studio with over 25 years of experience deploying emerging technologies to solve complex business challenges. A Duke University computer science graduate, Don has built his career at the intersection of strategy and execution—leading teams through successive technology waves from mobile and AR/VR to AI.

A leading voice in the AI community, Don brings deep subject matter expertise in building real-world integrations using agents. His recent technical deep-dive on Model Context Protocol (MCP)—widely regarded as one of the most comprehensive available—reflects the hands-on knowledge he brings to the workshop. Don combines the builder’s perspective of someone actively deploying agent systems with the strategic lens required to guide leaders through high-stakes decisions.

# New addition to the family!

We’re thrilled to announce something new on the horizon: the very first edition of the [Dutch AI Conference](https://aiconference.nl/), taking place on 11 and 12 March 2026 in Amsterdam.

This brand-new event joins the family of developer conferences alongside [Appdevcon](https://appdevcon.nl/), [Webdevcon](https://webdevcon.nl/) and [the Dutch PHP Conference](https://phpconference.nl/). It was created from a simple insight: while AI has rapidly become one of the most transformative forces in tech, there wasn’t yet a conference in the Netherlands where professionals could dive deep into the craft of building reliable and scalable AI solutions.

The Dutch AI Conference aims to change that. Here, AI engineers, data scientists, and software developers come together to learn from leading experts, exchange knowledge, and explore how to build responsible AI systems that truly make an impact.

## A familiar format, with a new focus

For those who’ve attended Webdevcon or its siblings before, the setup will feel familiar but with a fresh AI twist. This first edition will span two days:

- **Day 1:** hands-on workshops designed to boost your technical skills.
- **Day 2:** in-depth sessions where you’ll dive into architecture, scalability, and responsible AI practices.

The atmosphere? The same community-driven, high-quality event experience you know and love, now focused entirely on artificial intelligence.

## Call for Papers now open

Do you have a great story, research insight, or case study to share? The [Call for Papers](https://sessionize.com/dac-2026/) is now open. Whether you specialize in machine learning, LLMs, MLOps, or AI ethics, we’d love to hear from you.

We can’t wait to see how this new event will grow and bring together AI professionals eager to learn, share, and shape the future responsibly. Secure your [early bird ticket](https://dawntechnology.paydro.com/dac-2026) today!
